section_name: Obstruction of Operator’s View or Driving Mechanism
text:
(a) An operator may not drive a vehicle when it is loaded so that, or when the front seat has a number of persons, exceeding three, so that:

(1) the view of the operator to the front or sides of the vehicle is obstructed; or

(2) there is interference with the operator’s control over the driving mechanism of the vehicle.

(b) A passenger in a vehicle may not ride in a position that interferes with the operator’s view to the front or sides or control over the driving mechanism of the vehicle.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 545. Operation & Movement of Vehicles
##### § 545.417 — Obstruction of Operator’s View or Driving Mechanism

(a) An operator may not drive a vehicle when it is loaded so that, or when the front seat has a number of persons, exceeding three, so that:

    (1) the view of the operator to the front or sides of the vehicle is obstructed; or

    (2) there is interference with the operator’s control over the driving mechanism of the vehicle.

(b) A passenger in a vehicle may not ride in a position that interferes with the operator’s view to the front or sides or control over the driving mechanism of the vehicle.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: An operator may not drive a vehicle when it is loaded so that, or when the front seat has a number of persons, exceeding three, so that:
the view of the operator to the front or sides of the vehicle is obstructed; or
there is interference with the operator’s control over the driving mechanism of the vehicle.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: A passenger in a vehicle may not ride in a position that interferes with the operator’s view to the front or sides or control over the driving mechanism of the vehicle.

history.raw: Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.
